content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcal PnecrrcEs CoupussroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
March 25,2004
Roy A. Hanley, City Attomey
The Municipal Advocates Group, LLP
8930 Morro Road
Atascadero, CA93422
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-04-060
Dear Mr. Hanley:
This letter is in response to your request for written advice on thd conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act.l Your question concerns Ms. Yaeger's ability to
participate in a vote at the planning commission level on whether or not to recommend changes
to the Second Unit Ordinance in the City of Solvang.
Currently, the same or very similar issues are the subject of a pending investigation by
the Enforcement Division. Moreover, your question involves analysis of a series of interrelaied
decisions, some of which may have already occurred, such that issuing further advice at this time
would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective
administration of the Act so mandated by section 831 I l. Your request is therefore considered
withdrawn.
Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
General Counsel
Jwwjg
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear atTitle 2, sections 1g109 -
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
@

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Ms. Yaeger may participate in a planning commission vote on recommending changes to the Second Unit Ordinance in the City of Solvang under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice because a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division involves the same or similar issues, and the question involves analysis of interrelated decisions some of which may have already occurred, making advice on past conduct inconsistent with the impartial administration of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter (File No. W-04-060). The document contains no verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections but clearly identifies the subject matter and reason for withdrawal. The request was withdrawn rather than declined, indicating the FPPC deemed it appropriate to terminate the advice process mid-stream.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Ms. Yaeger may participate in a planning commission vote on recommending changes to the Second Unit Ordinance in the City of Solvang under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice because a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division involves the same or similar issues, and the question involves analysis of interrelated decisions some of which may have already occurred, making advice on past conduct inconsistent with the impartial administration of the Political Reform Act.

Currently, the same or very similar issues are the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Moreover, your question involves analysis of a series of interrelaied decisions, some of which may have already occurred, such that issuing further advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 831 I l. embedding.summary: FPPC withdrawal letter declining to provide written advice on whether a planning commissioner may vote on changes to a Second Unit Ordinance due to a related pending Enforcement Division investigation and the interrelated nature of past decisions involved.
